name: analyze_commits
description:
Analyzes local git commit history — authors, frequency, recent changes, and commit patterns. No API key required; reads directly from the local git repository.
Use when: user asks about who committed what, recent changes, commit history, activity patterns, or wants to understand how a local repo has evolved.
Scope: local git repository only — no network access. Run git commands via subprocess.
Not for: remote GitHub repo metadata (use get_repo_info), fetching remote files (use get_file), or searching code patterns (use search_code).

path.description: Path to the local git repository root. Example: '/Users/me/my-project'

since.description: Only show commits after this date. Accepts git date formats: '2024-01-01', '1 week ago', '3 months ago'. Optional.

until.description: Only show commits before this date. Accepts git date formats. Optional.

author.description: Filter commits by author name or email (partial match). Optional.

max_commits.description: Maximum number of commits to return. Default: 50.
